TimeJul 21, 2019 7:14 am
Comment
DITTO. ✊🏼 And also trying not to gender people in general, regardless of how passing they may seem to me. Like, for example, if I asked a stranger for directions and then I’m relaying those directions to someone else, I would say, “that person said we should go this way.” There’s no need to say man / woman.

faunie_s
TimeNov 05, 2018 7:45 pm
Comment
Yessssss! I’ve been vegan for 15 years and I’m never looking back. For me the secret to success is that I consider myself 95% vegan because sometimes I’m traveling and their are no options or I’m staying with a family in another country and they are making dinner and I don’t want to make it about me. And that’s ok! If I tried to go hardline 100% always, I probably wouldn’t have made it 15 years. I’d much rather do it 95% of the time for the rest of my life and 100% for a few years and then get burnt out. Anyway, that’s just my take on it. All the best!

eliduke
TimeJan 11, 2017 2:38 pm
Comment
I did seasonal work in Antarctica for 6 years and it feels like the words you're saying now are words I've said before. My first season not going back the The Ice was pretty difficult. Nothing in the real world could compare to my new life down there. My life back home at a certain boring normality to it. It definitely took some time to adjust, but I found new purpose and joy and life goes on!
